deepDives.title: Designing a *9-gate HITL topology* for governed agentic SDLC
deepDives.hook: When agents write, review, and test code on your behalf, governance can't be a checklist at the end — it has to be a control plane. A walkthrough of the 5-agent + 9-gate reference architecture, file-based handshake contracts, gate composition, and what happens when an agent disagrees with itself.

deepDives.title: Agentic AI for *STLC* on .NET
deepDives.hook: Why Microsoft Agent Framework over Python-native orchestration for a .NET-heavy test lifecycle — and what the trade-offs actually look like at scale.

deepDives.title: *Non-determinism* is the physics, not the bug
deepDives.hook: Stop treating LLM variance as a defect to engineer away. Treat it as the operating physics of the system and design test, observability, and governance around that.

deepDives.title: FinOps meets *Responsible AI*
deepDives.hook: An agent that costs $0.40 per task is interesting. An agent that costs $0.40 per task and runs 50,000 times a day is a P&L decision. Responsible AI without unit economics is theatre.

labs.title: IPLBuzz · *match prediction* engine
labs.hook: Production-grade rule-based statistical match prediction system with a multi-voice NLG narrative layer — no LLM in the prediction path by design. Built to study where rule-based engines still out-discipline LLMs.

labs.title: Pachaka Lokam · *meal planner*
labs.hook: Kerala-style household meal planning PWA with pantry-aware suggestions, weekly templates with smart substitution, and maid/milk delivery tracking. Shipped to the Play Store via TWA. Real users, real edge cases.

labs.title: ProfitPilot *v3* · NSE analysis pipeline
labs.hook: Backtest-gated picks pipeline with FinBERT sentiment, CANSLIM fundamentals, and a 20+ indicator rules engine. Deployed as a PWA. The lab where I work out how to keep an ML pipeline honest about its own failure modes.

labs.title: RVonWheelz · *community carpool*
labs.hook: Society carpooling app for a residential community — tab navigation, auto-destination filters, real-time matching. A study in keeping a small, useful product small and useful.

notes.title: When the *subagent pattern* outperforms the orchestrator
notes.hook: Three engagements in, the pattern is clear: file-based handshake contracts between subagents beat any in-memory orchestrator I've tried — for governance auditability alone. The orchestrator becomes the ceremony layer, not the brains.

notes.title: MCP vs A2A is the *wrong question*
notes.hook: They're complementary, not competing. MCP standardises how agents pick up tools. A2A standardises how agents talk to each other.

notes.title: A small win on a *review cycle*
notes.hook: Switched a multi-agent code review loop from "all agents weigh in" to "agents file disagreements, gate decides". Cycle time dropped 40%.

notes.title: Why I keep coming back to *rule-based* engines
notes.hook: The ML-enhanced version of ProfitPilot beats the rule-based version on average. The rule-based version beats it on the failure modes I care about.

notes.title: The *9th gate* is for the system, not the human
notes.hook: Originally added a final HITL gate at the docs stage thinking humans needed it. Turns out the gate exists so the system can stop itself — the human is just the witness.

systems.title: Autonomy *vs* control
systems.description: Every agentic system makes a topology choice — even if accidentally. A 2×2 for placing humans, agents, and stop conditions deliberately rather than by default.

systems.title: Layered *governance stack*
systems.description: Four horizontal layers separate policy from gates from topology from artifacts — so each can evolve without breaking the others. The shape behind a regulated SDLC agent program.

systems.title: *Multi-agent* telemetry pipeline
systems.description: Telemetry that captures intent, decision, and counterfactual — not just latency and token counts. Why APM tooling alone is structurally insufficient for autonomous systems.
